meta.title: Mercedes-Benz M120 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz M120 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Mercedes M120 is the flagship V12 that defined 1990s luxury — a masterpiece of engineering, but eye‑wateringly expensive to repair if neglected. Backed by real UK enquiries [ESTIMATED].

verdictRating.confidence: 80% confidence — based on owner reports and specialist workshop feedback across the classic Mercedes V12 range.

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: "The M120 is a bulletproof V12 masterpiece — but parts and labour are ruinously expensive on a budget — buy only with full history and a healthy contingency fund."
verdictRating.bestFor: Collectors and enthusiasts seeking the ultimate 1990s Mercedes V12 experience with deep pockets for maintenance.
verdictRating.avoidIf: You're on a tight budget or can't afford specialist V12 labour rates and parts costs.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M120 typically takes 16–22 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].

cta.label: → Compare M120 engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Cost — "How much does an M120 engine replacement cost?"
items.answer: A reconditioned M120 typically costs £6,000–£9,000 supply only [ESTIMATED], varying by specific variant (6.0L vs AMG 7.3L). Fitting adds £1,200–£2,500 depending on labour rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

items.question: Reliability — "Is the M120 a reliable engine?"
items.answer: The M120 is considered extremely robust for a V12 — its architecture is over‑engineered and many examples have covered 200,000+ miles. However, age‑related issues like cooling system degradation and oil leaks are common, and labour costs are high.

items.question: Common failure — "What is the most common M120 failure?"
items.answer: Timing chain guides and tensioners are the most critical weakness — they can fail and cause catastrophic valve/piston contact, typically from 80,000–120,000 miles. Cooling system components (water pump, radiator, hoses) are also frequent failure points on older cars.

items.question: Fitment — "Which Mercedes-Benz models use the M120?"
items.answer: The M120 was fitted exclusively to the flagship S600 (W140), CL600 (C140), and SL600 (R129) — the pinnacle of 1990s Mercedes luxury and performance. It was also used in the Pagani Zonda, but that's not a Mercedes‑badged model.

items.question: Longevity — "How long does the M120 last?"
items.answer: With meticulous maintenance and timely chain guide replacement, M120 engines can exceed 200,000–250,000 miles. Many examples are still on the road, but neglect quickly leads to very expensive repairs — budget accordingly.
